executiveSummary: All four user stories are implemented and all 11 dedicated tests pass on CPU-only CI. The bootstrap path (auto-download ComfyUI from GitHub when vendor/ComfyUI is absent) is fully functional: urllib.request/zipfile/shutil are used exclusively, the download is idempotent, error dicts are returned on failure with python_version always populated, and the README Quick Start section covers the required documentation. One minor naming deviation exists: FR-1 specified the constant name COMFYUI_PINNED_TAG but the implementation uses COMFYUI_PINNED_REF — semantically identical, but non-compliant with the exact identifier the PRD prescribed.

verificationByFR.notes: A module-level constant identifying the pinned ComfyUI release is present in _runtime.py, but it is named COMFYUI_PINNED_REF instead of COMFYUI_PINNED_TAG as the PRD specified.
COMFYUI_PINNED_ARCHIVE_URL is derived from COMFYUI_PINNED_REF and points to https://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI/archive/refs/tags/{ref}.zip as required.
Extraction strips the top-level ComfyUI-{ref}/ directory via glob('ComfyUI-*') + shutil.move, placing contents directly under vendor/ComfyUI/.
ensure_comfyui_available() calls _has_comfyui_runtime() and skips the download when vendor/ComfyUI already contains the comfy/ subdirectory. Confirmed by dedicated skip test.
Only urllib.request, zipfile, shutil, pathlib, and tempfile (all stdlib) are used for network and archive operations.
python_version is populated in all return paths: healthy dict, _runtime_not_found, and _runtime_not_responsive. Confirmed by parametrized error-dict test.

verificationByUS.notes: AC01-AC05 all satisfied: _has_comfyui_runtime checks comfy/ subdirectory, download uses urllib+zipfile, pinned ref constant is present, healthy dict returned after bootstrap, tests pass.
AC01-AC04 all satisfied: exceptions caught inside check_runtime(), error dict with 'error' key returned, never raises, python_version always present, tests pass.
AC01-AC04 all satisfied: test_check_runtime_bootstraps_from_absent_vendor_comfyui_dir patches urlretrieve with a minimal zip fixture, asserts no 'error' key, runs on CPU, passes.
AC01-AC03 all satisfied: README Quick Start section shows check_runtime() as first call, explains automatic download, and shows error dict handling. All three README tests pass.

minorObservations: FR-1 naming divergence: constant is COMFYUI_PINNED_REF instead of the PRD-specified COMFYUI_PINNED_TAG. Both convey the same semantic; renaming is a low-risk cosmetic fix.

The _runtime_not_found error message still says 'Run: git submodule update --init' which is misleading now that auto-download is implemented. Callers may get confusing guidance if check_runtime() catches an unexpected internal error (not a download failure).

ensure_comfyui_on_path() does not call ensure_comfyui_available() — callers of ensure_comfyui_on_path() directly (not via check_runtime()) will not trigger the bootstrap. This is unlikely to cause issues in practice given the current public API, but is a latent inconsistency.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: The iteration is functionally complete and well-tested. The single spec deviation (COMFYUI_PINNED_REF vs COMFYUI_PINNED_TAG) is cosmetic. Recommended refactor actions: (1) Rename COMFYUI_PINNED_REF to COMFYUI_PINNED_TAG in _runtime.py and update all references including tests; (2) Update the _runtime_not_found error message to reflect that auto-download was attempted and failed, rather than instructing the user to run git submodule commands; (3) Optionally have ensure_comfyui_on_path() call ensure_comfyui_available() for internal consistency, or document clearly that it does not.
